**Project : Agentic AI Recruitment Pipeline.**

**Name : Sujan Khadka**

**Problem Statement**

I am an international student applying for summer internships. I need to send out a lot of applications just to stay competitive. And like most applicants, I get a lot of rejections. The frustrating part is not the rejection itself. It is that every single one says the same thing: "We have decided to move forward with other candidates." That is it. No reason, no feedback, nothing to work with.

At a recent career fair I asked a hiring manager about this directly. She was honest. They receive hundreds of applications per role and there is simply no time to give everyone individual feedback. I get it. But from the applicant's side, it means I have no idea if I am being screened out because of a missing skill, weak projects, lack of experience, or something else entirely. Without knowing what the gap is, I cannot fix it.

That is what motivated this project. The idea is to build an agentic AI pipeline that automates the recruiter side of the process, RAG-grounded scoring resumes, handling emails, and scheduling interviews, but with one key difference: when a candidate is rejected, the system sends a specific and honest explanation of why, not a form letter. And when a candidate is accepted, it automatically schedules the interview and generates a tailored question set for the hiring manager based on that candidate's actual profile.

**Agent Architecture**

The system begins when a recruiter uploads a resume and job description. Agent 1, the Review Agent, retrieves relevant context from a RAG knowledge base containing past successful hires, role rubrics, and rejection history, then scores the resume and produces a structured gap analysis object containing the candidate's strengths, skill gaps, and an overall score. This gap analysis is passed to the Agent 2, Orchestrator Agent, an LLM that also receives the recruiter’s accept or reject decision and reasons about which downstream agents to invoke. On the accept path, the Orchestrator delegates to Agent 3, the Communication Agent, which sends a personalized acceptance email and schedules an interview via Google Calendar MCP, and simultaneously to Agent 4, the Preparation Pack Agent, which reads the gap analysis and generates a tailored set of interview questions for the hiring manager based on the candidate's specific weaknesses. On the reject path, the Orchestrator delegates to Agent 5, the Rejection Agent, which reads the same gap analysis and writes a specific, honest rejection email explaining the actual deciding factor rather than a generic response. Every candidate gets a meaningful reply, every hiring manager gets a focused interview guide, and the recruiter's only job is to make the final call.

![][image1]

**Tool Plan**

The system integrates three tools, each called dynamically by agents based on reasoning rather than hardcoded application logic.

**Gmail MCP server** is used by Agent 3 (Communication Agent) to send a personalized acceptance email to the selected candidate, and by Agent 5 (Rejection Agent) to send the specific rejection email. The agent composes the message content first, then decides to invoke the tool only when the message is ready to send.

**Google Calendar MCP server** is used by Agent 3 (Communication Agent) to create an interview event and send calendar invitations to both the candidate and the hiring manager. The agent determines the event title, description, and attendees from the job context before invoking the tool.

**File I/O (local tool)** is used by Agent 4 (Preparation Pack Agent) to write the generated interview question set to a structured document. This keeps the prep pack as a reusable artifact that the hiring manager can reference before and during the interview.

**RAG Plan**

The RAG pipeline is integrated into Agent 1, the Review Agent, and serves as the primary grounding mechanism for resume scoring. Instead of evaluating a candidate purely against the job description text, the Review Agent retrieves context from a knowledge base of past hiring decisions to make scoring more accurate and company-specific.

The document corpus will include at minimum five document types: anonymized resumes of past successful hires, role-specific scoring rubrics defining what strong and weak looks like for each key skill, historical rejection summaries explaining why past candidates at similar score levels were declined, job description templates for the target role, and brief onboarding outcome notes on how past hires performed in their first 90 days.

Documents will be chunked at the paragraph level and embedded using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small. Embeddings will be stored in ChromaDB, which requires no external infrastructure and is straightforward to run locally. At runtime, Agent 1 embeds the incoming resume and job description, retrieves the top-k most similar documents using cosine similarity, and injects that context into its system prompt before scoring. This ensures the gap analysis reflects real hiring standards rather than generic keyword matching.

**Tech Stack**

| Component | Choice |
| :---- | :---- |
| Language | Python 3.11+ |
| Agent Framework | LangGraph |
| LLM | Claude Sonnet 4.5 via Anthropic API |
| Embedding Model | OpenAI text-embedding-3-small |
| Vector Store | ChromaDB (local) |
| Email Tool | Gmail MCP server |
| Calendar Tool | Google Calendar MCP server |
| Environment | python-dotenv |
